title: Asha's Starlight Adventure
synopsis: When little Asha's favorite stuffed elephant goes missing at bedtime, she feels scared and sad. With help from her family, she embarks on a brave nighttime adventure through the house to find Kavi, discovering that being brave doesn't mean not feeling scared.

pages.text: Asha loved bedtime stories with her stuffed elephant, Kavi. Tonight, Mama tucked her into bed, but something was wrong. Kavi was nowhere to be found! Asha felt scared and sad without her best friend.

illustration.description: A cozy bedroom with warm yellow walls and star-patterned bedsheets. Asha, a young South Asian girl with dark curly hair in purple pajamas, sits up in bed looking worried. Her mother, wearing a green sari-style nightgown, sits beside her with a gentle, concerned expression. The room has a soft lamp glowing and children's books on a nightstand.
illustration.alt: Asha looking sad in bed while her mother comforts her

pages.text: "Where is Kavi?" Asha asked with tears in her eyes. Mama gave her a warm hug. "Don't worry, beta. We'll find Kavi together. Sometimes being brave means asking for help when we're scared."

illustration.description: Close-up of Mama hugging Asha tenderly. Asha's face shows a mix of sadness and hope, with small tears on her cheeks. Mama has kind brown eyes and long black hair in a braid. The illustration uses warm oranges and soft blues, with a construction paper texture that feels comforting and safe.
illustration.alt: Mama hugging scared Asha in a comforting embrace

pages.text: Asha felt a little braver with Mama beside her. They searched the living room first. Papa was reading on the couch with their cat, Mira. "Have you seen Kavi?" Asha asked. Papa shook his head, but smiled encouragingly.

illustration.description: A warm living room scene with Papa, a bearded South Asian man in casual clothes, sitting on a red couch reading a book. A fluffy orange tabby cat curls beside him. Asha and Mama stand in the doorway, with Asha looking hopeful despite her worry. The room has soft lighting from table lamps and colorful family photos on the walls.
illustration.alt: Asha and Mama asking Papa about the missing elephant in the living room

pages.text: Next, they checked the kitchen where Dadi was making warm milk. "My brave granddaughter," Dadi said with a smile, "I saw Kavi this afternoon." Asha felt happy and excited. Maybe they were getting closer!

illustration.description: The kitchen scene shows Dadi, an elderly South Asian woman with silver hair in a bun and a cheerful floral apron, stirring milk at the stove. Asha's face lights up with happiness and hope while Mama stands supportively behind her. The kitchen has warm yellow walls, copper pots, and steam rising from the milk, creating a cozy nighttime atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Dadi in the kitchen giving Asha hope about finding Kavi

pages.text: They searched everywhere - under cushions, behind curtains, in toy boxes. Asha started to feel scared again. What if Kavi was lost forever? But Mama reminded her, "You're being so brave, Asha. Let's try one more place."

illustration.description: A montage-style illustration showing Asha and Mama searching different areas of the house. Asha looks determined but worried, peeking under couch cushions and behind colorful curtains. The scene uses bright primary colors with rounded, soft shapes. Construction paper texture adds warmth to the determined search scene.
illustration.alt: Asha and Mama searching through the house for the lost elephant

pages.text: In the laundry room, Asha heard a familiar sound - the washing machine! There, sitting safely on top in a basket of clean, warm clothes, was Kavi! Asha felt so happy and relieved that she laughed out loud.

illustration.description: The laundry room scene shows Asha's face filled with pure joy and relief as she spots Kavi the elephant sitting in a wicker basket on top of the washing machine. The elephant looks fluffy and clean among colorful folded clothes. Mama stands behind Asha, smiling warmly. The room has soft lighting and the washing machine has a gentle blue glow.
illustration.alt: Asha finding Kavi the elephant safe in the laundry room

pages.text: Back in bed, Asha cuddled Kavi tight. "I was scared without you," she whispered. Mama tucked them both in. "You were very brave tonight, Asha. Being brave doesn't mean you're not scared - it means you keep going even when you are."

illustration.description: A peaceful bedroom scene with Asha snuggled happily in bed, holding Kavi the elephant close to her chest. Both look content and sleepy. Mama leans over to give a goodnight kiss, and soft starlight streams through the window. The colors are warm purples, soft yellows, and gentle blues, creating a perfectly cozy bedtime atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Asha peacefully in bed with Kavi, getting tucked in by Mama

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Asha feel when she couldn't find Kavi?
questions.explanation: Asha felt scared and sad when she couldn't find her special elephant friend Kavi at bedtime.

questions.question: Who helped Asha search for Kavi?
questions.explanation: Mama helped Asha search for Kavi throughout the house, showing that asking for help when scared is okay.

questions.question: Where did they find Kavi?
questions.explanation: Kavi was found safe in the laundry room, sitting in a basket of clean, warm clothes on top of the washing machine.

questions.question: What did Mama say about being brave?
choices: Being brave means you keep going even when scared
questions.explanation: Mama taught Asha that being brave doesn't mean you're not scared - it means you keep going even when you are scared.

"You were very _______ tonight, Asha," said Mama. "Being brave doesn't mean you're not scared - it means you keep going even when you are." Asha cuddled Kavi and felt _______.
